content: Betting platforms typically model dynamic-option markets with a market/event table plus a child options/outcomes table (one row per selectable outcome, FK to market), rather than fixed columns per outcome. Two payout models exist: fixed-odds (odds locked at bet time per outcome, payout = stake x odds_at_time for the winning outcome, house profit baked into overround/vig across all outcomes at market creation) vs pari-mutuel/pool betting (all stakes on one outcome pooled, commission deducted, remaining pool split among winners of that outcome - payout not known until pool closes). For over/under specifically, it's modeled as a two-outcome market (Over/Under) parameterized by an admin-set threshold/line value, functionally identical in payout mechanics to binary Sim/Nao once threshold is fixed. For multiple-choice, payout differs from binary because more than one non-winning outcome exists; only wagers on the single actual outcome are winners, and losing wagers span N-1 buckets rather than 1.
